plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero.
Trim semiconductor exposure (SMH) to reduce risk.
Increase allocation to income ETF (QQQI) for stability.
Reduce broad index (SPY) to meet single name limits.
Buy inverse semiconductor ETF (TECS) for diversification.
Hold a significant cash buffer for safety.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation with lower volatility. Expected monthly return is around 1.5%, but drawdowns should be minimal. Significant cash holding provides a safety net.

plan: Reduce leveraged ETF exposure (SOXL, SPAL) to minimal.
Trim semiconductor holdings (SMH) and reduce broad index (SPY).
Increase allocation to income (QQQI) and AI/Software (CRWD).
Add exposure to the energy sector (XLE) for diversification.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer for flexibility.
This blend aims for risk-first growth.
balanced.read: This balanced approach seeks steady growth with controlled risk, targeting around 2.5% monthly. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding diversification. Drawdowns are expected to be moderate.

plan: Increase leveraged ETF exposure (SOXL) significantly.
Add inverse semiconductor ETF (SOXS) for short-term opportunity.
Boost allocation to top AI/Software performer (CRWD).
Trim broad index (SPY) and income (QQQI) to fund adds.
Reduce semiconductor holdings (SMH) slightly.
Maintain a small cash buffer for quick rebalancing.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for high growth, targeting ~4% monthly, by leaning into leverage and strong setups. Expect significantly higher potential upside and downside. Daily leverage decay and larger drawdowns are key risks.

plan: Sell all leveraged and high-beta single names (SOXL, SPAL, CRWD, SMH).
Maximize allocation to income ETF (QQQI) for distributions.
Increase broad index (SPY) for stable income generation.
Hold a substantial cash buffer.
Focus on consistent monthly income over capital appreciation.
This strategy prioritizes low drawdown.
income.read: This income-focused plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions, targeting around 1% monthly yield. It minimizes volatility and capital risk by exiting growth and leveraged positions. NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
